title: Nano-island-encapsulated cobalt single-atom catalysts for breaking activity-stability trade-off in Fenton-like reactions

概念_主学科.evidence: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have been increasingly acknowledged for their performance in sustainable Fenton-like catalysis.
Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have been increasingly acknowledged for their performance in sustainable Fenton-like catalysis.
However, SACs face a trade-off between activity and stability in peroxymonosulfate (PMS)-based systems.

概念_主学科.normalized: 纳米岛封装单钴原子催化剂
概念_主学科.evidence: Herein, we design a nano-island encapsulated single cobalt atom (CoSA/Zn.O-ZnO) catalyst to enhance the activity and stability of PMS activation for contaminant degradation via an "island-sea" synergistic effect.

The CoSA/Zn.O-ZnO/PMS system exhibits a remarkable selectivity in exclusively generating sulfate radicals (SO4•-), leading to a complete removal of various recalcitrant pollutants within a shorter period.
The design effectively breaks the activity-stability trade-off typically associated with SACs.

概念_主学科.normalized: 过硫酸氢盐高级氧化过程
概念_主学科.evidence: In recent years, the emerged heterogenous peroxymonosulfate-based advanced oxidation processes (PMS-AOPs) have gained recognition as effective means to generate reactive oxygen species (ROS) and eradicate refractory organic pollutants in wastewater.

In recent years, the emerged heterogenous peroxymonosulfate-based advanced oxidation processes (PMS-AOPs) have gained recognition as effective means to generate reactive oxygen species (ROS) and eradicate refractory organic pollutants in wastewater.
In this configuration, small carrier-based ZnO nanoparticles (the "islands") are utilized to confine and stabilize Co single atoms.
The expansive ZnO substrate (the "sea") upholds a neutral microenvironment within the reaction system.

材料科学.normalized: 纳米岛封装单钴原子催化剂
材料科学.evidence: Herein, we design a nano-island encapsulated single cobalt atom (CoSA/Zn.O-ZnO) catalyst to enhance the activity and stability of PMS activation for contaminant degradation via an "island-sea" synergistic effect.
In this configuration, small carrier-based ZnO nanoparticles (the "islands") are utilized to confine and stabilize Co single atoms.
The expansive ZnO substrate (the "sea") upholds a neutral microenvironment within the reaction system.
In the current SAC designs, carbon materials commonly act as supports to anchor single-atom catalytic sites.

环境科学技术.evidence: Herein, we design a nano-island encapsulated single cobalt atom (CoSA/Zn.O-ZnO) catalyst to enhance the activity and stability of PMS activation for contaminant degradation via an "island-sea" synergistic effect.

The CoSA/Zn.O-ZnO/PMS system exhibits a remarkable selectivity in exclusively generating sulfate radicals (SO4•-), leading to a complete removal of various recalcitrant pollutants within a shorter period.

Characterized by minimal leaching of active sites, robust catalytic performance, and low-toxicity decontamination, this system proves highly efficient in multiple treatment cycles and complex water matrices.
Persistent organic pollutants in wastewater present substantial risks to human health and ecosystems.

title: High-entropy alloy enables multi-path electron synergism and lattice oxygen activation for enhanced oxygen evolution activity

概念_主学科.evidence: Electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is key to several energy technologies but suffers from low activity.
Leveraging the lattice oxygen activation mechanism (LOM) is a strategy for boosting its activity.
We reveal that high-entropy alloys (HEAs) can efficiently activate the LOM through synergistic multi-path electron transfer.
enabling effective LOM at the Ni-Co dual-site.
These insights allow the design of a NiFeCoCrW<sub>0.2</sub> HEA that exhibits improved activity.
achieving an overpotential of 220 mV at a current density of 10 mA cm<sup>-2</sup>.
achieving an overpotential of 220 mV at a current density of 10 mA cm<sup>-2</sup>.
We analyzed the catalyst surface using Raman spectroscopy fol- lowing the i-t tests in both KOH and TMAOH solutions.
a peak observed at 506 cm−1 in the Raman spectrum is indicative of the A1g stretching mode of the Ni–O bond within Ni(OH)2.
the Raman spectrum identiﬁed a pair of peaks at 560 and 680 cm−1, which correspond to the Fe–O bond.
a single peak at 527 cm−1, attributed to the Ni/Co–O bond.
The appearance of three distinct peaks at 458, 755, and 952 cm−1 is characteristic of the presence of TMA+ cations10.
the evaluation of the key intermediate μ-OO and the electrochemical OER behavior under speciﬁc test conditions provided direct evidence for distinguishing the reaction mechanisms.

材料科学.evidence: These observations reinforce the idea that the OER kinetic mechanism is related to the compositional species and that the high-entropy structure promotes the initiation of an efﬁcient electrocatalytic LOM pathway.
Notably, the Raman spectrum for the NiFeCoCrW0.2 catalyst dis- plays three prominent peaks associated with TMA+ ions.
These peaks were exclusively observed on the surfaces of the NiFeCoCrW0.2, NiFeCoW0.2, and NiFeCoCr electrodes following the i-t tests conducted in the TMAOH electrolyte.

title: MXene-Assisted NiFe sulfides for high-performance anion exchange membrane seawater electrolysis

概念_主学科.normalized: 阴离子交换膜海水电解
概念_主学科.evidence: Anion exchange membrane seawater electrolysis is vital for future large-scale green hydrogen production
however enduring a huge challenge that lacks high-stable oxygen evolution reaction electrocatalysts
Herein, we report a robust OER electrocatalyst for AEMSE by integrating MXene (Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub>) with NiFe sulfides
Herein, we report a robust OER electrocatalyst for AEMSE by integrating MXene (Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub>) with NiFe sulfides ((Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub>@Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub>)
The strong interaction between (Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub> and Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> induces electron distribution to trigger lattice oxygen mechanism
and particularly prohibits the dissolution of Fe species during OER process via the Ti-O-Fe bonding effectively
Furthermore, the good retention of sulfates and the abundant groups of Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> provide effective Cl<sup>-</sup> resistance
(Ni,Fe)S<sub>2</sub>@Ti<sub>3</sub>C<sub>2</sub> achieves high OER activity (1.598 V@2 A cm<sup>-2</sup>)
and long-term durability (1000 h) in seawater system
Furthermore, AEMSE with industrial current density (0.5 A cm<sup>-2</sup>)
and Raney Ni cathode with electrolysis efficiency of 70%
